file_name: UNITEDNATIONALBANCORP_03_03_1999-EX-99-Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc..txt

perturbation.original_text: "We are excited about the opportunities available to us as a result of our new partnership with BISYS," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National. "During the past year we completed an assessment of our information processing needs going forward into the next century.
perturbation.changed_text: "We are excited about the opportunities available to us as a result of our new partnership with BISYS," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National. "During the past year we attempted to assess our information processing needs going forward into the next century.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'completed an assessment' to 'attempted to assess' introduces ambiguity about whether a proper assessment was actually done. This could be problematic if regulations require financial institutions to conduct thorough risk assessments before outsourcing information processing.
perturbation.law_explanation: 12 CFR § 30 Appendix B requires banks to conduct a comprehensive risk assessment and due diligence process before outsourcing functions like information processing. By stating they 'attempted to assess' the needs, it implies the assessment might not have been thorough, potentially violating this regulation.

perturbation.justification: The change from "completed an assessment" to "attempted to assess" creates ambiguity around whether a thorough assessment, as required, was actually conducted. The Law Explanation states that 12 CFR § 30 Appendix B necessitates a comprehensive risk assessment before outsourcing functions. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that this change could be problematic if regulations require thorough risk assessments. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to 12 CFR Part 30, further grounding this in legal code. Therefore, the modified text introduces an ambiguity that could potentially lead to non-compliance.
